# japanauto.ca

> Independent Canadian marketplace for used Japanese cars and salvage-yard donor cars. Aggregates listings from verified AMVIC- and OMVIC-licensed dealers across Toronto, Montreal, Vancouver, Calgary, Edmonton, and Ottawa.

Editorial: every regulatory or YMYL claim is fact-checked by an independent AMVIC- or OMVIC-licensed advisor before publication. See [/editorial-policy/](https://japanauto.ca/editorial-policy/) for sourcing rules.

## Marketplace
- [Used Japanese cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/used-cars/): full catalog across 9 brands and 6 Tier-1 cities.
- [Donor-car parts directory](https://japanauto.ca/parts/): salvage-yard donor cars listed by make, model, and city.
- [Verified Canadian dealers](https://japanauto.ca/dealers/): independent AMVIC and OMVIC-licensed sellers.

## Brand pages
- [Toyota — used cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/brands/toyota/): Toyota has been the top-selling Japanese automaker in Canada for over a decade, with the RAV4 leading the SUV segment and a Toyota plant in Cambridge/Woodstock, Ontario producing RAV4 and Lexus RX…
- [Honda — used cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/brands/honda/): The Honda Civic has been the best-selling passenger car in Canada for 25+ consecutive years, and Honda's Alliston, Ontario plant produces the Civic and CR-V for the North American market — meaning…
- [Nissan — used cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/brands/nissan/): Nissan is consistently a top-five seller in Canada, with the Rogue being the brand's volume SUV and Intelligent AWD available across most crossover trims — though Canadian buyers should specifically…
- [Mazda — used cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/brands/mazda/): Mazda's i-Activ AWD is standard on the CX-50 and available on most CX-5 trims, making it a direct alternative to Subaru for Canadian winter buyers, though pre-2014 models have a known body-rust…
- [Subaru — used cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/brands/subaru/): Subaru's Symmetrical Full-Time AWD is standard on every model except the BRZ, making it the only mainstream Japanese brand offering AWD across the entire lineup — uniquely aligned with Canadian…
- [Lexus — used cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/brands/lexus/): Lexus is built on Toyota mechanicals — including the RX assembled at the Cambridge, Ontario plant — giving Canadian buyers German-tier luxury with Toyota-tier reliability and access to a nationwide…
- [Acura — used cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/brands/acura/): Acura is built by Honda at the Alliston, Ontario plant (MDX historically, plus shared platforms with the CR-V and Civic), giving Canadian luxury buyers near-Honda parts pricing and dealer access — a…
- [Infiniti — used cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/brands/infiniti/): Infiniti is Nissan's luxury division, sharing engines and platforms with the Pathfinder and Maxima — meaning Canadian buyers get true V6 luxury at depreciation-driven used prices, with parts sourced…
- [Mitsubishi — used cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/brands/mitsubishi/): Mitsubishi offers the strongest factory warranty among Japanese brands sold in Canada — 10 years or 160,000 km on the powertrain — and the Outlander PHEV is one of very few plug-in hybrid SUVs with…

## Editorial — buying guides, regulations, model deep-dives
- [AMVIC Rules for Used Car Buyers in Alberta: A 2026 Guide](https://japanauto.ca/blog/amvic-alberta-used-car-buyer-guide/): The Alberta Motor Vehicle Industry Council (AMVIC) regulates every licensed automotive business in Alberta. Buyers should verify any dealer's AMVIC license number before purchase via amvic.org's online lookup. AMVIC…
- [GST, HST, and PST on Used Cars in Canada: 2026 Tax Guide for Buyers](https://japanauto.ca/blog/gst-hst-pst-used-car-canada-out-of-province/): Used car taxes in Canada vary sharply by province in 2026: Ontario charges 13% HST on dealer and private sales (assessed on Canadian Black Book wholesale value, not sale price). British Columbia applies 12% PST on…
- [How to Buy a Used Toyota Camry in Calgary: A 2026 Buyer's Guide](https://japanauto.ca/blog/how-to-buy-used-toyota-camry-calgary/): A used Toyota Camry in Calgary typically lists between $14,000 and $26,000 CAD for 2016-2022 model years, with hybrids commanding a $2,000-$3,500 premium. Alberta buyers pay 5% GST and no PST, but must verify the seller…
- [How to Decode Toyota, Honda, and Nissan OEM Part Numbers](https://japanauto.ca/blog/how-to-decode-japanese-car-part-numbers/): Toyota, Honda, and Nissan use distinct OEM part number formats. Toyota numbers follow a 5-5-2 pattern (e.g. 04465-33471-00 for brake pads), Honda uses a 5-3-3 format with platform codes, and Nissan combines a base…
- [What's New in 2026 for Japanese Used Cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/blog/japanese-cars-2026-used-market-canada/): The 2026 Japanese used car market in Canada is shaped by three forces: continued tight supply of 2018-2020 models pushing prices 8-12% above pre-2020 norms, Toyota's RAV4 hybrid redesign making 2022-2023 used examples a…
- [OEM vs Aftermarket Parts for Used Hondas and Toyotas in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/blog/oem-vs-aftermarket-parts-honda-toyota-canada/): OEM Honda and Toyota parts in Canada cost 30-80% more than aftermarket equivalents, but for safety-critical components (brake calipers, suspension, airbag modules) OEM is the safer choice. Many aftermarket parts from…
- [Is a Used Subaru Outback the Best Japanese AWD for Canadian Winters?](https://japanauto.ca/blog/subaru-outback-canadian-winter-review/): The 2016-2022 Subaru Outback is one of the most capable Japanese AWD wagons for Canadian winters, with symmetrical full-time AWD and 8.7 inches of ground clearance. Used prices in 2026 range from $16,000 (2016 2.5i) to…
- [Active Transport Canada Recalls Affecting Japanese Used Cars in 2026](https://japanauto.ca/blog/transport-canada-recalls-japanese-cars-2026/): Transport Canada operates a free public recall database at defects.tc.gc.ca where any vehicle owner can search by VIN, year, make, or model. Active 2026 recalls disproportionately affect fuel-pump and…
- [Pre-Purchase Inspection Checklist for Used Japanese Cars in Canada](https://japanauto.ca/blog/used-japanese-car-pre-purchase-inspection-canada/): A pre-purchase inspection (PPI) on a used Japanese car in Canada costs $120-$250 CAD and takes 60-90 minutes at an independent shop. Focus checks on undercarriage rust (especially rocker panels and rear subframe), CVT…
- [Used Toyota Camry Hybrid (2016-2022): Real Canadian Fuel Economy](https://japanauto.ca/blog/used-toyota-camry-hybrid-canada-fuel-economy/): A used 2016-2022 Toyota Camry Hybrid delivers 5.7 to 6.4 L/100km combined under Canadian NRCan ratings, but real-world Canadian winter consumption rises 15-25% in -20°C conditions. Hybrid battery packs typically last…

## Glossary — Japanese vehicle technology and Canadian regulations
- [ABS](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/abs/): An Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) is an active safety system that prevents wheel lockup during hard braking by rapidly modulating brake-line pressure, allowing the driver to maintain steering…
- [AMVIC](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/amvic/): AMVIC (Alberta Motor Vehicle Industry Council) is the provincial delegated authority that licenses and regulates automotive businesses and salespeople in Alberta under the Consumer Protection…
- [AWD](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/awd/): All-Wheel Drive (AWD) is a drivetrain configuration that delivers engine torque to all four wheels of a vehicle, either continuously or on-demand, to improve traction on low-grip…
- [CarFax](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/carfax/): CarFax Canada (formerly CarProof) is the dominant Canadian vehicle history report service, aggregating data from provincial registries, insurance claims, lien registries, police records, and odometer…
- [Catalytic Converter](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/catalytic-converter/): A catalytic converter is an exhaust-system emissions device that uses precious-metal catalysts (platinum, palladium, rhodium) to convert harmful tailpipe pollutants — carbon monoxide, unburnt…
- [CVT](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/cvt/): A Continuously Variable Transmission (CVT) is an automatic transmission that uses a belt or chain running between two variable-diameter pulleys to provide a seamless, infinite range of effective gear…
- [e-CVT](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/e-cvt/): An e-CVT (electronic Continuously Variable Transmission) is Toyota's planetary-gear power-split device used in the Hybrid Synergy Drive system, providing CVT-like behaviour without belts or pulleys…
- [EV](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/ev/): An Electric Vehicle (EV), more precisely a Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV), is a vehicle powered exclusively by electricity stored in an onboard rechargeable battery and driven by one or more electric…
- [EyeSight](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/eyesight/): EyeSight is Subaru's stereo-camera-based driver assistance system that provides adaptive cruise control, pre-collision braking, lane departure and sway warning, lane keep assist, and lead-vehicle…
- [Hybrid](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/hybrid/): A hybrid vehicle is a vehicle that combines an internal-combustion engine with one or more electric motors and a battery, recovering energy through regenerative braking to improve fuel economy…
- [i-MMD](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/i-mmd/): i-MMD (intelligent Multi-Mode Drive), marketed in many regions as e:HEV, is Honda's two-motor hybrid powertrain that drives the wheels primarily via electric motor at most speeds, with the petrol…
- [i-VTEC](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/i-vtec/): i-VTEC (intelligent Variable Valve Timing and Lift Electronic Control) is Honda's variable valve-timing and lift system that combines VTEC's two- or three-stage cam-lobe switching with continuously…
- [JDM](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/jdm/): JDM (Japanese Domestic Market) refers to vehicles, engines, and parts originally manufactured for sale exclusively within Japan, typically built to Japanese regulatory and consumer specifications…
- [Kei Car](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/kei-car/): A kei car (軽自動車, kei-jidousha) is a class of Japanese microcar limited under Japanese tax and registration rules to maximum dimensions of 3.4 m × 1.48 m × 2.0 m and an engine displacement of 660 cc,…
- [Lien](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/lien/): A lien on a vehicle is a legal claim registered by a creditor — typically a bank, dealer financier, or judgment creditor — against the vehicle as security for an outstanding debt, which travels with…
- [OEM](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/oem/): An Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) part is a replacement component made by, or under contract to, the same manufacturer that produced the part originally installed on the vehicle at the…
- [OMVIC](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/omvic/): OMVIC (Ontario Motor Vehicle Industry Council) is Ontario's delegated administrative authority that regulates motor vehicle dealers and salespersons under the Motor Vehicle Dealers Act,…
- [PHEV](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/phev/): A Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV) is a hybrid vehicle with a larger traction battery that can be recharged from an external electrical source, providing meaningful all-electric driving range…
- [Provincial Safety Inspection](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/provincial-safety-inspection/): A provincial safety inspection is a government-mandated mechanical inspection of a vehicle's safety-relevant systems — brakes, suspension, steering, lights, tires, exhaust, and structural condition —…
- [RIV Program](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/riv-program/): The Registrar of Imported Vehicles (RIV) is a Transport Canada programme that ensures vehicles imported into Canada meet federal safety standards under the Motor Vehicle Safety Act, including…
- [S-AWC](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/s-awc/): S-AWC (Super All Wheel Control) is Mitsubishi's integrated vehicle-dynamics system that combines AWD, Active Yaw Control, Active Stability Control, and ABS to manage longitudinal and lateral vehicle…
- [Salvage Title](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/salvage-title/): A salvage title (Canadian provincial 'salvage' or 'rebuilt' brand) is a registration status assigned to a vehicle that an insurer has declared a total loss due to collision, fire, flood, or theft…
- [SH-AWD](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/sh-awd/): SH-AWD (Super Handling All-Wheel Drive) is Acura's torque-vectoring all-wheel drive system that distributes torque not only between the front and rear axles but also between the left and right rear…
- [SkyActiv](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/skyactiv/): SkyActiv is Mazda's umbrella brand for a suite of efficiency-focused engineering technologies covering engines (SkyActiv-G petrol, SkyActiv-D diesel, SkyActiv-X spark-controlled compression…
- [Symmetrical AWD](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/symmetrical-awd/): Symmetrical All-Wheel Drive is Subaru's longitudinal-engine, full-time AWD system in which the powertrain components are arranged symmetrically along the vehicle's centreline, providing balanced…
- [Timing Belt](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/timing-belt/): A timing belt is a toothed reinforced rubber belt that synchronises the rotation of an engine's crankshaft and camshafts so that the valves open and close in correct timing with each piston's…
- [UVIP](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/uvip/): A Used Vehicle Information Package (UVIP) is a mandatory Ontario government document that the seller of a privately sold used vehicle must provide to the buyer, containing the vehicle's registration…
- [VIN](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/vin/): A Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) is a 17-character alphanumeric code assigned to every motor vehicle since 1981 under ISO 3779, uniquely identifying that specific vehicle's manufacturer,…
- [VSA](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/vsa/): The Vehicle Sales Authority of British Columbia (VSA) is the delegated administrative authority that regulates retail motor-vehicle dealers and salespersons in British Columbia under the Motor Dealer…
- [VVT-i](https://japanauto.ca/glossary/vvt-i/): VVT-i (Variable Valve Timing with intelligence) is Toyota's variable valve-timing system that continuously adjusts intake camshaft phasing relative to crankshaft position to optimise torque, power,…

## Editorial team and policy
- [Editorial team](https://japanauto.ca/editorial-team/): bylined editors and licensed reviewer disclosures.
- [Editorial policy](https://japanauto.ca/editorial-policy/): sourcing rules, fact-checking process, correction policy.
